vision: Standalone Tempo blockchain primitives for the Elixir ecosystem.

cartouche_migration.description: Switch internal signing/recovery from Signet to Cartouche (ZenHive's signet fork).

dialyzer_hygiene.description: PLT scope (apps_direct) and path (priv/plts) tweaks for memory and survival across mix clean.

extraction.description: Initial extraction of Tempo primitives from MPP into standalone library.

faucet_polish.description: Polish work on the public faucet — replace fixed-sleep settle with poll loop.

hex_release.description: Publish v0.1.0 to Hex; switch downstream consumers from path dep to published version.

integration_coverage.description: Moderato integration suite, Builder gas-limit bump, MPP migration closure (v0.1.1).

public_faucet.description: Promote test-only Moderato faucet helper to public Onchain.Tempo.Faucet module.

task.title: Extract Tempo primitives from MPP
task.body: Initial extraction of Tempo blockchain primitives from MPP (Machine Payments Protocol) into a standalone Elixir library. Built `Onchain.Tempo.TIP20` (selectors, calldata encoders, DEX address), `Onchain.Tempo.Transaction` (0x76 struct, RLP deserialization, payment matching, fee-payer co-signing on 0x78 domain), `Onchain.Tempo.Transaction.Builder` (build + sign 0x76 — transfer, multicall, fee-payer variants), `Onchain.Tempo.RPC` (JSON-RPC: broadcast async/sync, fetch + parse receipt), `Onchain.Tempo.Transfer` (TransferWithMemo event log parsing), and the `OnchainTempo` root module with Descripex Discoverable. 77 unit tests covering deserialization, calldata encoding, payment matching, call scope validation, RPC stubs, and builder validation.
task.implemented: Extracted TIP20, Transaction, Transaction.Builder, RPC, Transfer, and the OnchainTempo Discoverable root module from MPP into a standalone library with 77 unit tests. Shipped as v0.1.0.

task.title: Publish to Hex
task.body: Publish onchain_tempo to Hex as v0.1.0 so downstream consumers (MPP and friends) can depend on a published version instead of a path dep. After publish, `onchain` switched from path dep to published version. See CHANGELOG.md v0.1.0.
task.implemented: Published onchain_tempo v0.1.0 to Hex; onchain switched from path dep to the published version.

task.title: Integration tests against Moderato testnet
task.body: Opt-in `:integration` suite at `test/onchain/tempo/integration/moderato_test.exs` exercising the full Builder → RPC pipeline against live Moderato (chain `42_431`, `https://rpc.moderato.tempo.xyz`). Three tests: Builder real nonce fetch + 0x76 round-trip via `Transaction.deserialize/1`, `RPC.fetch_receipt/2` returning `{:error, "Transaction not found on-chain"}` for unknown hashes, and `RPC.broadcast_sync/2` confirming a self-transfer of pathUSD with a real receipt + logs. New `test/support/moderato_faucet.ex` helper self-funds a fresh keypair per test via the Moderato `tempo_fundAddress` custom JSON-RPC — no env var required. Fresh keypair per test avoids nonce races; loud `flunk/1` on funding failure per project critical-rules.
task.implemented: Added opt-in :integration suite at test/onchain/tempo/integration/moderato_test.exs (3 tests: Builder round-trip, fetch_receipt not-found, broadcast_sync self-transfer) against live Moderato, plus a self-funding test/support/moderato_faucet.ex helper. Shipped in v0.1.1.

task.title: Raise Builder default gas_limit to 500_000
task.body: `Onchain.Tempo.Transaction.Builder` `@default_gas_limit` bumped from 200_000 to 500_000 — a stock TIP-20 transfer on Moderato consumes ~272k, so the previous default under-provisioned real transfers. Integration test no longer needs a per-call `gas_limit` override. 500k is a ceiling, not an amount spent — no real-world cost change; just prevents `out of gas` for the common TIP-20 path.
task.implemented: Bumped Builder @default_gas_limit from 200_000 to 500_000 to cover the ~272k a stock TIP-20 transfer consumes on Moderato. Shipped in v0.1.1.

task.title: Update MPP to use onchain_tempo
task.body: Bookkeeping closure — verified the MPP-side migration was already accomplished during the original v0.1.0 extraction (MPP Task 23, 2026-03-28). `MPP.Methods.Tempo` aliases `Onchain.Tempo.{RPC, Transaction, Transfer}`; no `MPP.Tempo.Transaction` module remains in `mpp/lib/`. Remaining `MPP.Tempo.*` modules (`Store`, `ConCacheStore`, `Methods.Tempo.SessionReceipt`) are MPP-specific concerns and intentionally stay in MPP. No code changes; the roadmap entry was stale.
task.implemented: Verified MPP migration was already complete (MPP Task 23, 2026-03-28); MPP aliases Onchain.Tempo.{RPC,Transaction,Transfer}. No code change — closed the stale roadmap entry. v0.1.1.

task.title: Replace Faucet fixed-sleep settle with poll loop
acceptance_criteria: fresh_funded_wallet/1 returns as soon as the funding tx is confirmed on-chain (not after a fixed sleep)
Integration suite remains green on Moderato (faucet still funds reliably)
Poll loop respects a configurable timeout / max-iterations to avoid hanging on slow blocks
Unit tests can still skip the wait by passing the existing :settle_ms shortcut (or equivalent zero-poll-time option)
task.body: `Onchain.Tempo.Faucet.fresh_funded_wallet/2` currently sleeps a fixed `@default_settle_ms` (2_500ms) after funding before returning. Replace with a poll loop on `eth_getTransactionCount` or `eth_getBalance` so the helper returns as soon as the funding transaction lands (Moderato blocks ~500ms; the fixed sleep wastes ~2s per integration test). Discovered during the v0.1.1 integration test work and carried forward when the helper was promoted to a public module in v0.2.0.
task.implemented: Replaced the Faucet fixed @default_settle_ms sleep with an eth_getBalance poll loop bounded by a configurable timeout / max-iterations; unit tests retain the zero-wait shortcut. Shipped in PR #1.

task.title: Public `Onchain.Tempo.Faucet` helper for `tempo_fundAddress`
task.body: Promoted the previously test-only `Onchain.Tempo.TestSupport.ModeratoFaucet` (`test/support/moderato_faucet.ex`) into public `Onchain.Tempo.Faucet` at `lib/onchain/tempo/faucet.ex` so downstream consumers writing their own integration suites can reuse the Moderato faucet recipe. Public API: `rpc_url/0` (Moderato default, `TEMPO_RPC_URL` override), `fund_address/2` (thin wrapper around `tempo_fundAddress` JSON-RPC, returns `{:ok, [tx_hash]}`), `fresh_funded_wallet/1` (generates a 32-byte keypair, funds it, sleeps for settlement). Both accept an opts keyword list with `:rpc_url`, `:req_options`, and (for `fresh_funded_wallet/1`) `:settle_ms`. Registered in `OnchainTempo`'s Descripex.Discoverable module list. Integration suite migrated; old test-support helper deleted; new unit tests at `test/onchain/tempo/faucet_test.exs` cover happy/error/transport paths via Req.Test stubs.
task.implemented: Promoted the test-only ModeratoFaucet to public Onchain.Tempo.Faucet (rpc_url/0, fund_address/2, fresh_funded_wallet/1) registered in OnchainTempo's Discoverable list, with Req.Test-stubbed unit tests. Shipped in v0.2.0.

task.title: Migrate signing to Cartouche
task.body: Switched internal signing/recovery aliases from `Signet.Signer.Curvy` / `Signet.Recover` to `Cartouche.Signer.Curvy` / `Cartouche.Recover` in `Onchain.Tempo.Transaction` and `Onchain.Tempo.Transaction.Builder`. Cartouche is ZenHive's fork of signet — drop-in compatible, available transitively via `onchain`. Tightened the `onchain` dep from `~> 0.5` to `~> 0.5.3` to ensure the cartouche-bearing version is resolved. Public API unchanged — purely internal refactor for consumers.
task.implemented: Switched signing/recovery aliases from Signet.Signer.Curvy / Signet.Recover to Cartouche.Signer.Curvy / Cartouche.Recover in Transaction and Transaction.Builder; tightened the onchain dep to ~> 0.5.3. Public API unchanged. Shipped in v0.2.1.

task.title: Dialyzer configuration (apps_direct + priv/plts)
task.body: Adopted `plt_add_deps: :apps_direct` to keep the PLT scoped to direct deps (tidewave/bandit's HTTP stack bloated the tree to ~800 modules and caused OOM kills on PLT builds). Moved PLT files from `_build/dialyzer/` to `priv/plts/` so they survive `mix clean` / `_build` wipes. Added `/priv/plts/` to `.gitignore`.
task.implemented: Adopted plt_add_deps: :apps_direct to scope the PLT to direct deps (avoiding OOM on the bloated tidewave/bandit tree) and moved PLT files to priv/plts/ (gitignored) so they survive mix clean. Shipped in v0.2.1.

task.title: Stop hardcoding Builder @default_gas_limit — estimate gas per-tx (mirror mppx)
acceptance_criteria: Builder estimates gas via eth_estimateGas when :gas_limit is omitted, with a headroom multiplier
A cold-storage TIP-20 transfer / transferWithMemo on Moderato no longer OOG-reverts at the default
An explicit :gas_limit override is still honored unchanged
Interim: if estimation is deferred, @default_gas_limit is raised to >= 1_000_000 with a comment citing the ~560k measured cost
out_of_scope: Adding the eth_estimateGas RPC wrapper itself (tracked in onchain's roadmap)
Faucet funding changes — empirically pathUSD + gas both land before tempo_fundAddress returns; no race (Task 6 poll loop already sufficient)
task.body:
`Onchain.Tempo.Transaction.Builder` `@default_gas_limit` (currently 500_000, raised from 200_000 in Task 4 for the then-"~272k" cold TIP-20 transfer) is stale again: a cold-storage TIP-20 transfer on Moderato now measures ~560k, because the chain charges a protocol fee (a Transfer to the `0xfeec…` collector) on the transfer path. At the 500k default these transfers OOG-revert (`gas_used == gas_limit`, status 0) — empirically confirmed: identical transfer reverts at 400k/500k, succeeds at 2M using 560019 gas. This is the second time a static default went stale; the constant chases a moving on-chain number.

Mirror the reference SDK (mppx sizes every transaction via `eth_estimateGas`, not a constant): have the Builder estimate gas per transaction (consuming the `eth_estimateGas` helper being added to `onchain` — cross-repo dependency) with a safety headroom multiplier, instead of a fixed default. Keep an explicit `:gas_limit` override working. As an interim until estimation lands, raise `@default_gas_limit` to >= 1_000_000 (> the measured 560k, < the mppx fee-payer `maxGas` ceiling of 2_000_000) so cold transfers stop reverting.
task.implemented: Builder estimates gas per-tx via Onchain.RPC.eth_estimate_gas (1.25x headroom) when :gas_limit omitted; per-call summed, errors propagate, explicit override honored. Probed Moderato: cold eth_estimateGas=807k captures the protocol fee, so standard estimation suffices. onchain floor ~> 0.9. 21 offline + 4 live Moderato tests (incl. cold transfer confirms), Builder 100% cover.

task.title: Update transport stub off the :cartouche,:client seam after onchain's Req migration
acceptance_criteria: It stubs the onchain RPC transport via onchain's post-migration mechanism and passes
mix.exs onchain requirement covers the migrated onchain version
out_of_scope: The onchain transport migration itself (its own roadmap task — must ship first)
task.body:
Follow-on to onchain's transport migration (onchain roadmap task: "Migrate HTTP transport off cartouche's removed Finch seams"). cartouche 0.5.0 dropped the `config :cartouche, :client` seam and onchain is moving its own transport off it; once onchain ships, `test/onchain/tempo/transaction/builder_estimate_test.exs` no longer works — it stubs the onchain RPC transport by toggling `Application.{get,put,delete}_env(:cartouche, :client, module)` (its `setup`/`restore_client` helpers, lines ~44-51, plus the `async: false` comment at the top explaining the global-seam toggle).

After onchain ships its new transport-stub seam (Req.Test / function plug, or onchain's own override key), rewrite this test's stubbing to use that mechanism instead of `:cartouche, :client`. Bump the `{:onchain, ...}` requirement in mix.exs to the version that ships the migration if onchain raises its version. Keep the test's intent unchanged (it asserts builder gas-estimate behavior against a stubbed RPC).
